item.text: {"summary":"Propose one distinct initial feature-search direction: semi-supervised graph label-propagation features built from combined labeled train and unlabeled test covariates, evaluated with the same simple balanced model panel used for other feature families.","hypotheses":[{"title":"Transductive Graph Label-Propagation Features","summary":"Add fold-safe semi-supervised class-probability features by propagating train labels across a KNN graph built on combined train/test photometric covariates.","feature_family":"semi_supervised_graph_label_features","feature_strategy":"Build a compact standardized space from ugriz magnitudes, redshift, adjacent/broad color indices, alpha/delta, and one-hot spectral_type and galaxy_population. For each CV fold, construct a sparse KNN similarity graph over fold-train rows, fold-validation rows with labels masked, and optionally test rows as unlabeled covariate-only anchors; run LabelSpreading or a lightweight equivalent label-propagation routine with validation labels set to -1. Materialize OOF features for each row: propagated class probabilities, max probability, class margin, entropy, neighbor-label agreement, and distance-weighted labeled-neighbor coverage. For final test features, rerun with all training rows labeled and test rows unlabeled. Do not use validation/test labels, id, or direct target encodings.","baseline_model_panel":"Use the standard simple balanced panel: a regularized multinomial linear classifier on scaled features, a shallow class-weighted tree ensemble, and a class-weighted gradient-boosted tree model such as LightGBM or CatBoost with balanced class handling.","model_panel_rationale":"The propagated probabilities are compact, smooth manifold features that should be directly usable by a linear model while tree models can test nonlinear interactions with raw photometry and categories; balanced weighting keeps the panel aligned with balanced accuracy without making the experiment about tuning.","validation_strategy":"5-fold StratifiedKFold on train with strict masking of fold-validation labels during label propagation; report balanced accuracy overall and by class, and compare each panel model with and without the graph features using identical folds.","materialization_hint":"Implement this as a staged transformer before model fitting: create base photometric/color/categorical matrix, scale numeric columns on fold-train only, fit a sparse KNN-kernel LabelSpreading-style transform with validation/test labels set to -1, append only the resulting probability/uncertainty/margin columns to the ordinary feature table, then train the baseline panel.","expected_signal":"If useful, CV should show a visible lift for the linear model and improved minority-class recall, especially for QSO/STAR boundary cases; high-confidence low-entropy propagated regions should align with better per-class accuracy, while noisy or disconnected graph regions will show weak margins and little model gain.","novelty_confidence":"medium","risk":"This partially overlaps with prior local-class-posterior and unsupervised-locus ideas, but differs by using transductive graph smoothing over labeled and unlabeled covariates rather than direct KNN class counts or unsupervised clusters. Main risks are leakage if fold-validation labels are not masked, public/CV mismatch from including test covariates, and memory/runtime pressure on roughly 824k combined rows unless a sparse KNN graph or sampled anchors are used.","sources":["https://scikit-learn.org/stable/modules/semi_supervised.html","https://scikit-learn.org/stable/modules/generated/sklearn.semi_supervised.LabelSpreading.html","https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.18218","https://arxiv.org/abs/1904.04717"]}]}
